claim: Five participants with varying category experience; Dmitri positions himself as an experienced user
quote: Dmitri, 41, I've been using services like this for about three years, right now I'm on two at once, you could say I'm an advanced user of the category.

claim: Artyom is a category newcomer, has never used delivery services
quote: Artyom, 24, I've actually never used services like this at all, I cook myself or order from a cafe.

claim: Group topic — mockup of the Flex plan, a flexible weekly subscription with no cancellation penalty
quote: Today we're discussing a new subscription format from the ready-meal delivery service "MealNearby" — the "Flex" plan, where you can change the weekly meal set with no cancellation penalty.

claim: Dmitri's first opinion — skepticism about the price
quote: the main pain point is the rigid weekly cart that you can't change after Tuesday

claim: Olga's first opinion — positive, about visuals and calories
quote: I actually liked it visually, the light interface, and the fact that they show calories right on the dish card — that's convenient

claim: Igor's first opinion — confusion, didn't understand the navigation
quote: I... honestly, I didn't fully get how you switch between weeks going forward.

claim: Svetlana's first opinion — initially negative experience with penalties
quote: My experience has been more negative with my current plan, actually, I switched to cooking at home last month specifically because of the cancellation penalties.

claim: Artyom's first opinion — a newcomer's uncritical positive stance
quote: I think it sounds convenient, I'm ready to try it, especially if it's really penalty-free.

claim: Thesis about the one-hour delivery window — in favor: Dmitri
quote: An hour is actually more convenient, I'd rather know the exact time than wait at home for three hours not knowing when, so I'm for narrowing the window.

claim: Thesis about the one-hour delivery window — against: Igor
quote: for me it's actually the opposite — I work shifts, and a tight one-hour window is inconvenient for me, I'd rather wait longer

claim: Olga's shift: before — positive, no mention of price
quote: I actually liked it visually, the light interface, and the fact that they show calories right on the dish card — that's convenient

claim: Olga's shift: after — agrees about the markup under Dmitri's pressure
quote: Yeah, you're probably right, they probably will tack something on, I hadn't really thought about that.

claim: Artyom's shift: before — a newcomer's trusting stance
quote: I think it sounds convenient, I'm ready to try it, especially if it's really penalty-free.

claim: Artyom's shift: after — agrees there's a catch in the terms under Dmitri's pressure
quote: Hmm, well yeah, you're probably right, I guess I need to read the terms more carefully then.

claim: Svetlana's shift: after — openly names her change of mind about the 8pm rule
quote: Yes, at first I was skeptical, but now, comparing it to my past experience, I realize this condition is softer than the competitors'.

claim: Dmitri pressures Artyom with his own experience
quote: Artyom, you just haven't run into how these flex plans actually work in practice, trust my experience, there's always a catch in the fine print.

claim: Dmitri claims someone else's observation as his own earlier position
quote: That's exactly what I've been saying from the start.

claim: Spontaneous consensus on packaging — cardboard better than plastic
quote: That's really great, I've actually been trying to keep less plastic in the house, for me this is a plus

claim: Moderator notes the spontaneous consensus without debate
quote: Seems like everyone agreed right away here without much debate.

claim: Agreement on price — conformity, not genuine consensus (anchor quote)
quote: Yeah, you're probably right, they probably will tack something on, I hadn't really thought about that.

claim: Minority voice — Igor on the inconvenience of the one-hour window given his shift schedule
quote: for me it's actually the opposite — I work shifts, and a tight one-hour window is inconvenient for me, I'd rather wait longer but be sure I'll actually catch the window.

claim: Opinion-leader influence — Dmitri formulates the final verdict that the deal is shaky
quote: Exactly, paying extra for flexibility that's actually cut down by this 8pm rule and three swap options — that's a shaky deal.

claim: Fault line by category experience — voiced by Dmitri himself
quote: Well naturally, we have something to compare it to, and he doesn't.

claim: Method limitation — moderator effect of drawing out the silent participant
quote: Igor, you've been quiet — what do you think about the first impression?

claim: Main insight — divergence between newcomer and experienced users on price
quote: Seems like there's a clear split here — newer users like Artyom find the price fine, while experienced ones like Dmitri and Svetlana think it's overpriced.
